However, concern grew when neither Rodney nor Donald responded to calls or text. Driving by the house, Rodney's sister noticed a newspaper on the ground outside the door. After his arrest, Rodney was charged with second-degree murder with intent, not premeditated. His bail was set to $1 million. Less than a week later, his lawyers imposed rule 20. Rule 20 involves a process where mental health professionals observe the defendant while they are jailed to determine if they were aware their crime was morally wrong at the time of the event, and if they are mentally cognitive enough to have a fair and just trial. His court case was also moved from a jury trial to a bench trial, meaning the court would determine a verdict based strictly on fact and professional expertise of the case, rather than jury opinion. During his evaluation, court documents revealed that Rodney viewed himself as a victim in this case. When asked about the events that took place, he stated, now that I've read the reports, I feel like it's not fair to ask me that. It's like putting me here with things and I don't think it's fair to ask me those questions. They're holding me here on probable cause, which feels like I'm guilty until proven innocent. It's this terrible tragic thing, and I feel sad, but I'm certain I never intended for something like this to happen. I've never hurt anyone.

That could be one of the triggers conceivably. I think it was a number of things. There really wasn't a trigger. It was just out of nowhere. In another instance, he described short-term memory loss of the accident and stated, my mind's still kind of sketchy. After evaluation, doctors found Rodney quote, meets diagnostic criteria for major depressive disorder, recurrent, severe, in full remission, with anxious distress and psychotic features. Based on his statements and mental diagnosis, it was determined Rodney was having a psychological episode at the time of the alleged offense and that he did not know the wrongfulness of his conduct. This was further supported by a known history of Rodney's where he would experience a rapid onset of symptoms, followed by a compulsion to act. Both instances of his no suicide attempts involved him cutting himself, according to court documents, also implying a pattern. Forensics would later determine that Rodney's DNA and fingerprints were found on the murder weapons in question, and that he carried through the acts of killing his own father beyond a reasonable doubt.

However, the court found that Rodney did not show signs he was planning the attack by acquiring weapons, and he did not attempt to hide the crime afterwards. It wasn't until hours later that he showed knowledge that his actions were wrong. Due to these facts of the case, the court acquitted Rodney of his second-degree murder charge, and he would be considered not guilty. In the meantime, the court ordered that Rodney would be detained in a state hospital or other facility pending completion of the proceedings. Prosecutors are required by state law to file a petition for civil commitment after a not guilty verdict of this nature. Following due process and input from medical professionals, a judge will decide whether Rodney should be civilly committed. That process is still going through the system, and the court trial will be held on August 30th, 2024. Rodney's attorney shared that he has been restored to competency through treatment and struggles knowing he killed his dad.
